'''Jordan Cox''' (27 May 1992 – 30 April 2020) was an English [[rugby league]] footballer who last played for [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> His main position was {{rlp|pr}}.

He had also played in the [[Super League]] for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Warrington Wolves]]. He had also appeared for [[Newcastle Thunder]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] outside of the Super League.

==Career==

Cox made his first team debut for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] on 8 May 2011 away to [[York City Knights]], in the  [[2011 Challenge Cup]]. On his début he looked confident and managed to get himself onto the scoresheet twice, after making his debut in the [[Challenge Cup]] he then went on to make a further 6 appearances in the [[Super League]] competition, earning himself a call up to the [[England Knights]] squad, which he later had to pull out of for post-season surgery.

At the start of the 2012 season, Cox played in 8 of the first 12 games earning himself a regular spot in the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] starting 17, until the beginning of May.
When during an U20s game, his spleen ruptured causing him to miss 3 months of the season. However, although it was initially thought of as a season-ending injury, he quickly progressed through the rest and rehab stages and later made his comeback against the [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://hullkr.co.uk/jordan-cox-28 |title=Hull KR - Official Web Site |accessdate=2011-05-09 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110612180915/http://hullkr.co.uk/jordan-cox-28 |archivedate=12 June 2011 | publisher = Hull KR official website }}</ref>

Between 2013 and 2015 Cox played for [[Newcastle Thunder]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and the [[Huddersfield Giants]] on loan. After being signed, he made 18 appearances for the [[Warrington Wolves]]; again in the [[Super League]].

Cox penned a one-year deal with [[Kingstone Press Championship|Championship]] outfit [[Sheffield Eagles]] in January 2017.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/10730049/sheffield-eagles-sign-ex-warrington-prop-jordan-cox|title=Sheffield Eagles sign ex-Warrington prop Jordan Cox|work=Sky Sports|date=15 January 2017|accessdate=22 January 2017}}</ref> After playing 9 games in 2017 for the Sheffield Eagles, Cox joined [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] side [[York City Knights]] on loan until the end of the season. He scored on his debut for them in a 28-18 home victory against [[Workington Town]]. At the end of the [[2017 RFL Championship|season]] Cox was released by the Sheffield Eagles.

==Personal life==
Cox died at his home in Hull on 30 April 2020, [[27 Club|aged 27]].<ref>{{cite news |date= 30 April 2020 |title= Jordan Cox: Ex-Hull KR & Warrington player dies, aged 27 |url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/52493603 |work= BBC Sport |access-date= 6 May 2020}}</ref> No cause of death was made public at the time of his death, but the circumstances were described as "non-suspicious".<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1390/statement-jordan-cox|title=Statement: Jordan Cox|work=Super League|date=30 April 2020|accessdate=30 April 2020}}</ref>  An inquest in January 2021 was informed that death was due to an accidental drug overdose.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Shaw |first1=Matthew |title=Jordan Cox died of accidental drug overdose |url=https://www.totalrl.com/jordan-cox-died-accidental-drug-overdose/ |access-date=8 January 2021 |work=TotalRL.com {{!}} Rugby League Express {{!}} Rugby League World |date=8 January 2021}}</ref>
